next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 9

The needle went in wrong the first time. Kem felt the resistance of Dren's skin, the rubbery give of it, and his hand jerked sideways. Dren hissed through his teeth.

"Sorry. I'm sorry."

"Just do it," Dren said. "Don't talk about it."

Kem repositioned the needle, a curved sewing needle Vel had bent with pliers somewhere in the first week of travel and kept in a cloth wrap with waxed thread. The wound ran from Dren's forearm almost to the elbow, a torn opening where silo shrapnel had ripped through sleeve and flesh. The blood had slowed to a dark seep.

They had made the creek because Vel kept giving orders and Kem kept obeying them fast enough to matter. Two levels went on a Concealment Charm when blood in the shallows turned the crossing into a bright red trail marker. Three more went on a Strength Charm when Dren slipped climbing the far bank and almost tore the wound wider. Kem had hauled him the last stretch with Dren's good arm over his shoulders and the borrowed force burning through his thighs. By the time they'd found this clearing and Vel had washed the gash out with creek water and spirits, five more levels were gone.

Now it was Kem's turn. Vel couldn't do it. The ribs she'd cracked in the fight made the job impossible once she had to lean in and hold the skin together. She had tried, stopped after the first attempt, and braced an elbow against her side while she breathed around the pain.

"Straight through," she'd told him, showing the motion in the air. "Both sides, same depth. Pull it snug, not tight. You're closing skin. Not tying off a sack."...
open_hooks_to_carry: Comedy of untrained agents causing accidental destruction
The shift from funny to dangerous: raw power hurts regardless of skill
Vel forced to overspend — cracked ribs from a glancing hit
Dren's silo-shrapnel wound as collateral damage
Sable watching and learning rather than fighting
prev_tail_excerpt:
"How much have you spent since you found me? Total."

She considered. "Three. Outrider fight. Nothing since."

"So you're still at twenty-eight."

"Yeah."

Kem checked his own Ledger. The number sat at the edge of his awareness and came into focus when he reached for it: 225. He'd been 300 eleven days ago. Seventy-five levels gone. Vel had spent three in the same stretch, and all of them with people trying to kill them.

"I used a Flame Charm two nights ago," he said. "Because the flint was damp."

"I know."

"That was nearly a level."

"Close."

"That was a patch of wall for her."

Vel poked the fire. A log shifted and dropped a spray of sparks.

"Yeah," she said.

He waited. She fed another stick to the fire. Nothing else came.

The healing ward's pulse faded as the evening deepened. Somewhere in the settlement a door thudded shut. A dog barked once. Insects took the grass around them.

When the dark finished moving in under the trees, Kem kept his hands in his lap. He did not call a Light Charm. He sat there until the ring went dull on his finger.
secondary_character_beats: Dren's injury is not heroic — he is hit by debris from an explosion nobody aimed, which is how civilians get hurt in ability crossfire
state_deltas_required: Five untrained level-300 agents — raw power is both absurd and devastating when wielded without skill
Vel forced below budget; Dren wounded and invested; Sable learns by watching; incompetence at 300 is dangerous and getting less incompetent
